name: BTC 5m Compression Fakeout Reclaim
edge_rationale: A compressed 5m range can store volatility. A close outside that range that quickly reclaims the range indicates a failed breakout and liquidity grab, which may create mean-reversion edge.
counterparty_or_market_mechanism: Breakout chasers and stop orders triggered outside a compressed range lose when the breakout fails and price reclaims the range.
frozen_assumptions: No production, PAPER, runtime, core, execution, or settings changes.
No parameter rescue after seeing results.
Entry occurs at first confirmation bar close.
Exit uses simplified TP/SL only, no trailing or partial exits.
expected_observation: If the setup works, it generates materially more trades than 15m trial-00095 while keeping ER above 1.0, PF above 1.5, acceptable drawdown, and positive cost sensitivity.
failure_modes: Compression definition is too broad and catches noisy ranges.
Reclaim confirmation arrives after the mean-reversion move already happened.
Trade count increases but extra events have lower expectancy.
Profits concentrate in a small number of months.
author: Codex
